questions: Did any round feature more than three strikes before ending?
Did either team lose points after a scoreboard update?
Was there a round where no points were added despite multiple correct answers?
Did any round end with fewer than two answers revealed?
Was there a round where all answers were revealed without any strike occurring?
Did the board ever decrease the number of revealed answers within a round?
In the first round, did the leftmost man on the right team represent first?
In the first round, was the lowest-scoring answer given by the leftmost man on the right team?
In the first round, did the team whose member buzzed first win the round?
In the first round, did the left team answer only one question correctly?
In the final result, did the all-male team fail to score any points?
Was the team that received the bicycle the team that won the first round?
Did the number of answers revealed on the board increase gradually across multiple turns before a strike occurred?
Was there a moment when the audience reacted before the scoreboard visibly updated?
Did a player give an answer that was not immediately shown on the board but later turned out to be correct?
Did the team that buzzed second in the first round gain control of the board in a later round after previously losing a steal?
Did the score difference first widen, then narrow, and then widen again before the final round?
Did the losing team temporarily take the lead before ultimately losing the game?
Was there a moment where both teams had scored in the same round but only one retained control?
Did the number of revealed answers increase to at least four before the first strike in any round?
Was there a steal attempt that occurred after exactly three strikes and resulted in additional points?
Did the team that lost the game win more rounds than they lost?
